page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled, delivering dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 768 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the n2d-highmem-96 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test places the instance in the top 10% of performance, while Passmark CPU encryption and physics tests also achieve top-tier rankings. Single-core performance, measured via Geekbench and stress-ng, falls within the top 25% to middle 50% range. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a strong cached read bandwidth of 5754.6 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write operations drop to average levels at 143.05 GB/sec and 61.43 GB/sec respectively.

Given its high resource density and memory capacity, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications. Passmark database benchmarks and Redis SET operations both register in the top 25%, confirming its efficiency for transactional systems. While it lacks hardware acceleration for heavy deep learning, it handles CPU-based LLM prompt processing for smaller models efficiently. The qualitative cost profile is favorable for memory-bound enterprise workloads that require dedicated, high-density RAM without the added expense of local storage or GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance configured with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 48 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU execution, particularly in encryption and physics simulations, alongside strong database and Redis transaction throughput. While single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth remain average, the instance delivers excellent cached memory read speeds. This profile makes the n2d-highmem-96 highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity without the overhead of specialized storage or accelerator hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors for high-density database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine delivering dedicated AMD EPYC processing power for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance in encryption and physics benchmarks
Strong database operations and Redis transaction throughput
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and databases
